296	- Follow-up `us_011_fix_silent_no_secret` rewrites the daily cron's body as a `do $body$ ... $body$` PL/pgSQL block that resolves both vault secrets first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` if either is NULL — fixes a Medium reviewer finding (pg_net silently returns success on `url := NULL`, so a missing vault secret would have logged as a successful run forever instead of surfacing in `cron.job_run_details`). Promoted both the pg_net NULL-trap and the vault-secret pattern to Codebase Patterns.
297	- Verified via `execute_sql` DO block: synthetic 25h-old `fresh`+`is_latest` snapshot flips to `stale` after running the marker body verbatim. Confirmed both jobs registered with `active=true` in `cron.job` (jobid 1 = mark-metrics-stale, jobid 2 = cron-refresh-metrics).
298	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417163000_us_011_cron_jobs.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` regenerated — byte-identical (cron jobs aren't surfaced in the public schema).
299	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)

file.content:
- **Forcing an unauthed app state for mobile-mcp verification requires BOTH app-terminate AND keychain reset before relaunch.** `expo-secure-store` is iOS Keychain backed; `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` only clears the keychain persistence layer, not the in-memory AuthProvider state of a running app. If the app is merely backgrounded, the `cachedToken` module-scope variable in `src/lib/storage.ts` + the React `user` state in `AuthProvider` both survive — and on foreground the AuthProvider does NOT re-read SecureStore (hydration runs once on mount). Correct sequence: `mobile_terminate_app(host.exp.Exponent)` → `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` → `mobile_launch_app(host.exp.Exponent)` → tap the project in Expo Go's Recently Opened. The re-launch then mounts a fresh AuthProvider which hydrates from the (now empty) keychain and renders the role picker. Required for any story that verifies an auth-gated screen by logging out from outside the app (US-033 duplicate-username 409 test, and whenever US-037/US-038 UI lands before a sign-out-in-app flow exists).
- **mobile-mcp `type_keys` drops the first character or two of some inputs non-deterministically.** Observed on iPhone 17 Pro sim (iOS 26.3) with Expo Go — e.g. `u33_demo_17` → `u3_demo_17`, `dup-attempt@example.com` → `d-attempt@example.com`. If the typed value needs to match exactly (e.g. duplicate-conflict test), verify via `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` after each field and correct using `mobile_double_tap_on_screen` (triggers iOS word-selection menu) → tap "Select All" → retype. Don't rely on visual screenshot inspection to confirm input — the kerning hides a dropped char.
- **Defense-in-depth CHECK constraints mirror edge-function input regexes.** Edge functions that validate user-shaped strings (handles, usernames, slugs) via a `const RE = /^.../` before inserting are the first gate, but service_role writers (admin scripts, later migrations seeding fixtures, future edge functions reusing the same table) bypass that validation. Add a matching `CHECK (col::text ~ '<same-pattern>')` constraint on the column so the shape guarantee becomes a database invariant instead of a caller-by-caller convention. Before applying, run `select ... where col !~ '<pattern>'` and fix offenders — the ALTER will fail otherwise. Ref: `social_links_handle_format_chk` in `us_035_social_links_handle_check.sql` mirrors `HANDLE_RE` in `manage-social-link`/`auth-signup-creator`. Applies to any new user-string column (lister company slug, campaign slug, etc.).
- **RLS self-read policies must cover EVERY status the UI needs to surface, not just the happy path.** The `metric_snapshots_self_read` policy originally required `status='fresh'`, but us_011's hourly cron flips `is_latest` rows from `fresh` → `stale` in place — so the creator's "Stale" chip query returned zero rows (RLS silently filtered) even when the snapshot legitimately existed. Rule: audit every status enum on any RLS-gated table against the UI states that need to render it, and widen the policy accordingly. Keep `service_role`-only states (in-flight `refreshing`, error `failed`) off the self-read policy — those states are communicated to the client through edge-function responses, not direct table reads. Ref: `us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale.sql`.
- **TabBar missing from a11y tree on some screens — use coord-clicks as the verified workaround.** `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` doesn't always report the custom `TabBar` (src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx) tab buttons inside nested route groups (observed on `/feed` during US-035 verification). The buttons are rendered and tappable, but absent from the accessibility tree snapshot. Workaround: take a screenshot to visually confirm the tab bar is present, then `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` at the known tab positions (on iPhone 17 Pro sim: ~y=820, x ≈ 44/130/217/303/352 for a 5-tab bar — inspect the screenshot for exact coords per layout). Don't block UI verification on missing a11y entries; confirm via screenshot + tap.
- **Supabase Realtime requires an explicit `supabase.realtime.setAuth(token)` before `channel().subscribe()` under the custom-JWT wrapper pattern.** `src/lib/supabase.ts` overrides `global.fetch` with `marketifyFetch` so HTTP requests carry our HS256 `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` token — but Realtime opens a separate websocket (`/realtime/v1`) that does NOT go through the fetch wrapper. Without `setAuth`, the websocket either auths as anon (no rows visible under RLS) or fails handshake entirely. Pattern: `await supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()); channel = supabase.channel(...).on('postgres_changes', ...).subscribe();` then `removeChannel(channel)` in cleanup. Gate the whole block on a `cancelled` flag because setAuth is async and the effect may re-run or unmount before it resolves. Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` US-036 realtime effect.
- **Synchronous `useRef` lock for rapid-fire gesture handlers where React state deps would stale-capture.** When a `useCallback` has `[stateFlag, ...]` in its deps and is invoked by a gesture/press that can fire multiple times in the same render tick, the closure captures the PREVIOUS `stateFlag` — two pulls in a row both see `false` and both run. Pattern: pair a `useRef(false)` that the callback reads and writes synchronously (`if (lock.current) return; lock.current = true; ... finally { lock.current = false }`) with the React state that drives UI. Remove the state flag from the callback's deps since the ref is the actual guard. Same stale-capture mechanism as Pattern #82's Reanimated worklets applies to rapid-fire JS-side handlers (pull-to-refresh, double-press submits, scroll-triggered loads). Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` `refreshLock` in US-036.
- **Pan gesture coexisting with a parent ScrollView: use `activeOffsetY([15, 9999])` + `failOffsetX([-20, 20])` instead of `activateAfterLongPress`.** Long-press activation is unreliable when the user starts dragging quickly — ScrollView's native gesture wins and the Pan never fires. The threshold-based activation per RNGH canonical docs declares "only downward pulls past 15pt count as pan" without stealing all touches: upward scrolls and horizontal drifts are rejected, so the ScrollView handles normal content scroll, and a deliberate downward pull triggers the Pan. `failOffsetX` prevents a diagonal swipe from being misread as a pull. Inside `.onUpdate`, apply a piecewise-linear damping past threshold (`raw <= THRESHOLD ? raw : THRESHOLD + (raw - THRESHOLD) * 0.35`) for the rubber-band feel. Ref: `RefreshablePlatformCard` in `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`.
- **Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` bundles files exactly as you name them.** The entrypoint MUST be named `source/<entrypoint>.ts` (not `index.ts`) and the `entrypoint_path` field must match (`source/index.ts`). Shared modules live at bundle root as `_shared/<name>.ts` — NOT `source/_shared/<name>.ts`. The entrypoint imports them as `../_shared/<name>.ts` because the bundler resolves relative to `source/`. Mismatched layouts produce "Module not found `_shared/<name>.ts`" at deploy time with no other hint. Canonical reference: any sibling under `supabase/functions/*/index.ts` paired with the deploy `files: [{name: "source/index.ts", ...}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts", ...}, ...]` — `metrics-refresh` is the earliest example, `get-listing-detail` follows the same layout.
- **When a story's AC conflicts with `docs/tech-architecture.md`, the spec wins — but additive fields are defensible when the spec is under-specified.** Ralph rule #1 is "specs win," and that holds for response envelopes, DB column shape, and anything the spec is explicit about. But specs often leave ambiguity at the field level — e.g. §5.4's `failed_conditions: Array<{ metric; required; actual }>` elides `platform` while `min_followers` is ambiguous across TikTok/Instagram. Pattern: align the envelope shape to the spec, but enrich with additive fields when the UI can't disambiguate from spec alone. Document the addition in the edge function's header comment block so future iterations see the rationale. Ref: `get-listing-detail/index.ts` returning `failed_conditions: [{metric, platform, required, actual}]` as a documented extension.
- **Concurrency via version-id pin + FOR UPDATE re-read, not engine duplication in PL/pgSQL.** When an edge function needs to "re-evaluate eligibility against the NOW-current listing version" (spec §5.5) without duplicating the TS eligibility engine in SQL, the sanctioned split is: (1) edge fn reads `listings.current_version_id` and runs TS eligibility against it, then (2) calls an RPC that re-reads the same listing under `select ... for update`, compares the locked `current_version_id` to `p_expected_version_id`, and returns `LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` on mismatch so the client re-opens the detail view. The row lock serializes this flow against `app_private.bump_listing_version` (which runs under its own row lock on the listings UPDATE). Invariant depends on the bump trigger producing a new `current_version_id` for every eligibility-affecting mutation — if a future trigger change lets a condition edit land without bumping, the RPC would silently commit against stale TS eligibility. Document the dependency in the RPC header. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417270000_us_041_apply_to_listing_rpc.sql` + `apply-to-listing/index.ts`.
- **Fail-closed applies to malformed data, not just missing data.** Spec §3d's fail-closed rule covers the "null actual metric" case (user hasn't been scraped), but the same principle extends to unreadable THRESHOLD values: a `gte` pre-condition with a NULL or non-numeric `numeric_threshold` must fail the check, not silently pass. Silently returning `null` from the single-condition evaluator for a malformed row is how a future mis-migrated condition flips a listing to "always eligible" without any alarm. Rule: every branch of an eligibility-or-permission evaluator that hits unreadable input returns a fail, never an early "skip." Schema CHECKs should prevent the malformed row from existing, but the evaluator is the last line of defense. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` `evaluatePreCondition`.
- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).
- **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is unreliable inside an RN `<Modal>` (and therefore inside our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens its own native window, so KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame disagree and the contents do not get pushed up. Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.
- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.
- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. This is both a cost optimization (don't burn TikTok rate-limit slots on requests that will 4xx regardless) and a DDoS-amplifier guard (an unauth POST must not be able to force an upstream fetch). Split the error codes: `500 SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` / `500 DB_ERROR` for our-fault failures, `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` for upstream unavailability — clients that auto-retry benefit because upstream outages are usually retryable, DB bugs rarely are. Document BOTH codes in the contract comment. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` oEmbed section + error envelope header.
- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
- **PostgREST embeds silently NULL joined columns when the target table's RLS filters the caller out — always audit every `.select('..., related!fk(col)')` embed against the related table's RLS policies.** Failure mode: the embed syntax succeeds, returns rows, and populates the parent columns; the embedded field just comes back as `null` or `[]` under RLS because PostgREST applies the policies per-row transparently. Highest-risk embeds on this project: (a) `users!lister_id(username)` where `users_self_select` is self-only — every cross-tenant handle read (creator viewing lister handle, lister viewing applicant handle) will null out; (b) `listing_versions!...(...)` where `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active' OR caller=lister` — any historical/closed listing version the caller doesn't own will null; (c) `listings!...(title)` where `listings_public_read` requires `status='active'` — same closed-listing nullout. Rule: before writing a PostgREST embed, check the target table's RLS; if the policy wouldn't return the specific row under the caller's role, replace the embed with a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does its own ownership check. DO NOT widen the self-only policy to "return usernames if handle is public" — that leaks the full row shape; DEFINER + narrow column projection is the correct primitive. Ref: Codebase Pattern #40 (`submission_reuse_count`), Codebase Pattern #110 (`apply_to_listing_rpc`), and `public.list_my_applications()` from US-043.
- **SECURITY DEFINER "list-my-rows-with-foreign-context" RPC skeleton.** Contract: `returns table(<flat columns including the cross-tenant bits>)`, `language sql stable security definer set search_path = ''`, body is a straight `select ... from public.<owned_table> t left join public.<foreign_table> f on ... where t.<owner_col> = public.current_user_id() order by ...`. Grants: `revoke all ... from public`, `revoke all ... from anon`, `grant execute ... to authenticated`. Service-role is intentionally not granted — it already has BYPASSRLS and wouldn't use this shape. Why `stable`, not `volatile`: the function reads but doesn't write, so PostgREST can plan around it and query planner caches can reuse results within a statement. Why `sql` over `plpgsql`: the body is one statement, no control flow, no GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS — inlines better. Verified pattern via a transactional `begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.<fn>();` block that confirms (a) the DEFINER bypass returns the cross-tenant columns, (b) the ownership check still gates other users' rows (swap the sub claim to a different creator and assert zero rows). Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`. Applies to any future "my X with lister/creator/reviewer context" list screen.
- **Screen-RPC naming convention: `public.list_my_<things>` for caller-scoped list reads, `public.<action>_rpc` for mutations, `public.<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates.** Precedents: `public.list_my_applications()` (US-043) / `public.apply_to_listing_rpc(...)` (US-041) / `public.submission_reuse_count(uuid)` (US-009). Keep the `list_my_*` functions argumentless (the caller is implicit via `current_user_id()`) — passing a caller UUID would re-introduce the spoofing surface the helpers exist to eliminate. Keep them in the `public` schema (not `app_private`) because they need to be callable via PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`; `app_private` is reserved for server-side triggers + cron bodies. The `authenticated` grant is mandatory — without it PostgREST returns 42501 "permission denied for function" and the MCP `execute_sql` test path (runs as `postgres`) silently passes, masking the bug.
- **Segmented control a11y: `accessibilityRole="tablist"` wrapper + `accessibilityRole="tab"` on each button + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`**, NOT the Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `checked`. Chip is for multi-select filter rows where each pill is independent; a segmented control is single-select and semantically is a tab bar. iOS VoiceOver reads "tab, Pending, selected, 1 of 4" for the tablist shape — correct under Apple's a11y guidance. `selected` is the right state (not `checked`) because the user isn't toggling a boolean, they're picking one of N mutually-exclusive views. Inline the control rather than promoting to a shared primitive until a second screen needs it — premature abstraction hurts more than a ~40-line duplicate. Ref: `SegmentControl` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (US-043).
- **Enum-to-UI-bucket maps live as a static `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` next to the screen that owns the mapping, not in a shared constants file.** `application_status` has 6 values; the Applied tab shows 4 segments (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled), with `withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed` all bucketing to "cancelled" per design.md §3.1 label + the AC's 4-segment design. Type the map as `Record<Database['public']['Enums']['application_status'], Segment>` so adding a 7th enum value trips tsc at the map literal. Do NOT collapse the three cancellation variants in the DB (they carry distinct semantic meaning for future admin/analytics use); collapse only at the UI layer. Same pattern applies to any status enum whose DB shape is richer than the screen's tab count. Ref: `STATUS_BUCKET` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx`.
- **Two-phase verification probe pattern for screens whose populated case isn't reachable from the in-app DevRolePicker.** The DevRolePicker mints a literal `dev-creator-token` string that PostgREST rejects with PGRST301; only `auth-signup-creator`-issued JWTs are accepted. For screens that need populated-row screenshots (US-043 list-my-applications, future US-047 my-submissions), the project precedent is: (1) take empty-state mobile-mcp screenshots from the DevRolePicker session to validate layout + segment switching + each empty copy, (2) run a two-phase Bun probe that signs up a fresh creator (mode=signup → prints `{user_id, token}`), pauses for the operator to seed fixtures via Supabase MCP execute_sql, then (mode=query) calls the screen's RPC with the captured JWT and asserts the contract holds end-to-end through PostgREST. The probe **must mirror the screen's bucket-collapse map exactly** (typed `Record<EnumValue, Bucket>`, not a chained ternary with an implicit else) so a future enum addition trips tsc on both sides. Always delete the probe fixture (user + applications + cascading social_links/creator_profiles/metric_snapshots) via execute_sql immediately after the probe finishes; the user row is not auto-cascaded. Ref: `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts`. Same posture US-036 took for the gesture-blocked refresh test.
- **Realtime `postgres_changes` without a server-side filter when the table lacks a direct caller-id column.** `applications` has `creator_id` so US-044's subscription filters on `creator_id=eq.<uuid>`; `submissions` has no such column (ownership runs through `submissions.application_id → applications.creator_id`). For tables gated via a join, OMIT the filter clause entirely and let the broker's RLS evaluation (`<table>_creator_rw` / `_lister_read` policies) gate visibility server-side. The alternative — passing `application_id=in.(...)` — would require the client to maintain the id set, re-subscribe on every new owned row, and race the RLS evaluation; strictly worse than letting RLS do its job. Keep the client-side row-match guard (`rowsRef.current.find((r) => r.id === next.id)`) so updates for rows that aren't currently surfaced by the RPC are ignored cheaply. Applies to any child-of-join table: `submission_videos`, future `review_threads`, etc. Ref: `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx` US-047 realtime effect.
- **Supabase Realtime tenant hibernation is an infrastructure-class blocker, not a code defect.** When `supabase.realtime.channel(...).subscribe()` returns `TIMED_OUT` repeatedly (4+ times) and `get_logs({service:'realtime'})` shows `UnableToConnectToProject: Realtime was unable to connect to the project database` every 5-15s, the project's Realtime tenant is stuck in an initialization loop that can stretch `Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection` past 8 minutes before the broker gives up and the subscription never reaches `SUBSCRIBED`. The HTTP side (PostgREST, edge functions, `execute_sql`) stays healthy throughout — this is purely a realtime-broker-to-replication-slot connectivity issue. The `pg_stat_activity` view shows realtime's own connections as `idle`, giving a false-positive "it's connected" signal; trust the realtime logs' `UnableToConnectToProject` entries over `pg_stat_activity`. For realtime-dependent stories (US-044), the decision rule: if empty-state mobile-mcp screenshots + code-reviewer pass + RLS/publication membership verified at DB level, ship the implementation under `passes:false` with a detailed blocker note + ready-to-run probe, rather than looping indefinitely. Next iteration re-runs the probe once logs show `Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (the postgres_changes slot, distinct from the `_messages_` slot that handles broadcast) without a trailing `UnableToConnectToProject`. Before investigating any realtime test failure, check these logs FIRST — client-side debugging is wasted effort when the broker can't reach Postgres. Ref: `scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts` + this iteration's US-044 blocker notes.

## 2026-04-18 02:45 — US-043 verification close-out
- JWT drift was resolved upstream by US-036's claim-structure fix (auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'); probe-jwt-drift.ts now returns 200 on PostgREST. Re-ran end-to-end mobile + HTTP verification to close US-043 cleanly without any code/migration changes.
- Mobile-mcp captured 4 empty-state screenshots (us-043-{pending,approved,rejected,cancelled}-empty.png) from the in-app DevRolePicker creator session — confirms segmented control + tap-switching across all 4 segments + each segment's bespoke empty copy renders correctly under iPhone 17 Pro (iOS 26.3) sim. Segment buttons are NOT in the a11y tree (similar to TabBar Pattern #104) — coord-tapped at PENDING(58,138) APPROVED(130,138) REJECTED(222,138) CANCELLED(78,178).
- New verification probe `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts` (two-phase signup/query): signed up a fresh probe creator via auth-signup-creator → captured a real Marketify JWT → seeded 4 applications for that creator via execute_sql (pending/Acme, approved/Luxe, rejected/Acme, cancelled_listing_edit/Luxe — same partial-unique-friendly distribution as the original us_043_seed_applications migration) → called /rest/v1/rpc/list_my_applications with the JWT → received 4 rows covering all 4 buckets, with non-null lister_handle (@acme_studio) + listing_title resolved on every row. Probe verdict: PASS. Test fixture deleted via execute_sql immediately after.
- Files changed:
  - `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-pending-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-approved-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-rejected-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-cancelled-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-043 passes:true + updated notes)
  - `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` (this entry + new Codebase Pattern)
- No app source / migrations / generated types touched in this iteration.
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no DDL changes since US-043 original commit; advisor state unchanged) / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. IMPORTANT (confidence 92): probe's `bucket()` collapsed `withdrawn → cancelled` via implicit else-fallthrough rather than an explicit map mirroring the screen's STATUS_BUCKET; replaced with a typed `Record<Row['status'], Bucket>` so a future 7th enum value trips tsc instead of silently bucketing as 'cancelled'. MINOR (confidence 85): JWT echoed to stdout in signup mode; addressed with an explicit comment documenting the short-lived TTL + immediate fixture-deletion expectation. NIT (confidence 80): `body: '{}'` vs `JSON.stringify({})` skipped — functionally identical, not worth churn. Verification posture (empty-state mobile + populated HTTP probe) explicitly endorsed as a defensible substitute for "screenshot each segment with seeded data" against the US-036/US-038 precedent.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The two-phase signup/query probe pattern is the canonical workaround for any screen whose DevRolePicker session can't reach populated rows. Promoted to Codebase Patterns. US-047 (my-submissions tab) will need the same shape — copy this probe's skeleton, swap `list_my_applications` → `list_my_submissions`, and remember to delete the fixture cascade explicitly (the user row is NOT cascaded to social_links/creator_profiles/metric_snapshots — the cleanup statement in this iteration's progress.txt entry is the canonical order).
  - The DB-vs-PostgREST verification distinction matters: SQL-level RLS verification (existing US-043 commit, transactional `set local role authenticated` block) proves the function body is correct, but only an HTTP probe under a real JWT proves the PostgREST gateway accepts it AND the function's grant chain to `authenticated` works in production. Both are needed for any RPC-backed screen.
  - When a screen's empty copy varies per state (US-043's 4 distinct empty messages per segment), screenshot each empty state — not just one — so the design.md §5.5 empty-copy contract is verified independently of any populated rendering. Cheap-to-take, high diagnostic value.
  - DO NOT commit a probe that prints credentials to stdout without a comment block explaining the TTL bound + the operator's responsibility to delete the test fixture. The pattern is acceptable for ephemeral dev probes, dangerous for anything that survives the run. This rule is now in the Codebase Patterns section above (two-phase verification probe pattern).

## 2026-04-18 00:38 — US-043

Idempotent via `on conflict (id) do nothing`.

- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Audit PostgREST embeds against the target table's RLS BEFORE writing the .select().** The silent-nullout failure mode is high-damage low-visibility: no error, no warning, just a `null` field. The checklist for this project specifically: `users!<fk>` cross-tenant reads are always silent-nulled by `users_self_select`; `listing_versions!<fk>` reads are silent-nulled for non-active listings; `listings!<fk>(title)` reads are silent-nulled for non-active listings. If your screen needs ANY of those cross-tenant fields, you need a DEFINER RPC, not an embed. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **The "list my rows with foreign context" RPC is a reusable skeleton.** argumentless, SQL-language, STABLE, DEFINER, `set search_path=''`, left-joined, filtered by `current_user_id()`, granted to authenticated only. Copy this skeleton for US-056 (creator inbox), US-054 (lister campaigns list), US-057 (lister applicants list) — all three have the same cross-tenant-read-under-self-only-RLS shape. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **Segmented controls are tablists, not checkbox groups.** When a control is single-select + mutually-exclusive, `accessibilityRole="tablist"` + per-button `"tab"` + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}` is the correct a11y shape. The Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `state={{ checked }}` is only correct for multi-select filters. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **DB enums can outgrow UI segments — a static typed Record bridges them.** `application_status` has 6 values; the tab shows 4 segments. A `Record<ApplicationStatus, Segment>` literal with all 6 keys explicitly enumerated means tsc catches the day a 7th enum value lands; collapsing the DB shape to match the UI would lose the distinction between user-initiated withdrawal and lister-initiated cancellation. Keep the DB rich, collapse at the UI. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **Skipping `passes:true` because of an upstream env blocker is the right call.** US-036 + US-038 + now US-043 are all mature implementations waiting on a dashboard-side JWT secret alignment. Pretending they're "done" would corrupt the progress signal for the human reviewer; pretending they're "not started" would lose the implementation work. The `passes:false` + detailed notes pattern (with re-run probe script + rationale) is how we keep the ledger honest. Same posture will apply to any future story whose verification path goes through PostgREST under the creator/lister JWT until the drift is resolved.

(b) §15b threshold triggers were intentionally scoped out of US-010; promoted to Codebase Patterns as a hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055.
- Spec gap: §15b threshold triggers (`refresh_listing_thresholds`, `refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump`) are NOT in US-010 — scoped to §15 cascade trigger only per AC. Without them, `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` go stale on conditions-only bumps. Tracked as a Codebase Patterns hard dependency.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - `text[] || 'literal'` is a silent time bomb in `search_path=''` functions — it parses fine (create-time), fails at first fire. Always cast literals to `::text` when appending to a text array inside hardened PL/pgSQL.
  - The `to_jsonb(new)` snapshot inside a BEFORE trigger captures the NEW record mid-mutation. If the trigger later sets `new.current_version_id` (or any other column), the snapshot reflects the pre-set value, not the final written state. Harmless when paired with dedicated columns (like `previous_version_id`) but worth knowing.
  - When a story intentionally defers part of a spec section, treat it as a hard dependency and promote it to Codebase Patterns so the next iteration that might ship something load-bearing on the deferred part sees it first. Migration-header comments alone aren't enough — iterations read Codebase Patterns first.
---

## 2026-04-17 16:35 — US-011
- Applied `us_011_cron_jobs` via Supabase MCP: enabled `pg_net` extension and scheduled two pg_cron jobs — `mark-metrics-stale` (hourly `0 * * * *`, verbatim from spec §16: flips `metric_snapshots.status` `fresh→stale` past 24h with `is_latest`) and `cron-refresh-metrics` (daily `0 4 * * *`: POSTs to the eventually-deployed `cron-refresh-metrics` edge function via `net.http_post`, reading URL + service-role key from `vault.decrypted_secrets`). Idempotent re-scheduling guards with `cron.unschedule` existence checks.
- Follow-up `us_011_fix_silent_no_secret` rewrites the daily cron's body as a `do $body$ ... $body$` PL/pgSQL block that resolves both vault secrets first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` if either is NULL — fixes a Medium reviewer finding (pg_net silently returns success on `url := NULL`, so a missing vault secret would have logged as a successful run forever instead of surfacing in `cron.job_run_details`). Promoted both the pg_net NULL-trap and the vault-secret pattern to Codebase Patterns.
- Verified via `execute_sql` DO block: synthetic 25h-old `fresh`+`is_latest` snapshot flips to `stale` after running the marker body verbatim. Confirmed both jobs registered with `active=true` in `cron.job` (jobid 1 = mark-metrics-stale, jobid 2 = cron-refresh-metrics).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417163000_us_011_cron_jobs.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` regenerated — byte-identical (cron jobs aren't surfaced in the public schema).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)

content.content:
{
  "id": "US-044",
  "title": "Realtime subscription for application status changes (creator)",
  "description": "Push status flips into the UI via Supabase Realtime.",
  "acceptanceCriteria": [
    "Subscribe to applications WHERE creator_user_id = me on the Applied tab",
    "On UPDATE: optimistically move the row to the new segment; fire toast 'Your application was approved/rejected'",
    "Mobile-mcp verification: from Supabase Studio (or via execute_sql) flip a seeded application to approved while the screen is open — confirm UI updates without manual refresh",
    "Commit"
  ],
  "priority": 44,
  "passes": false,
  "notes": "2026-04-18: Implementation complete + code-reviewed, but E2E realtime delivery verification BLOCKED by Supabase Realtime tenant infrastructure failure for project [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]. Server-side realtime logs show persistent 'UnableToConnectToProject: Realtime was unable to connect to the project database' every 5-15s (even after the probe stops), with one prior 'Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 494396 ms' (~8min) suggesting a stuck tenant cold-start path — this is beyond what the client can recover from. Work shipped this iteration: (1) migration us_044_applications_realtime_publication adds public.applications to supabase_realtime (default replica identity is sufficient because the client only reads payload.new.id+status); (2) app/(creator)/applications.tsx extended with a useEffect that calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()) then subscribes to UPDATE events on applications filtered by creator_id=eq.<me>, patches rows in state on a status change, and fires the AC-specified success/error toast ('Your application was approved/rejected') for approved and rejected only (other 4 enum values patch the row silently since AC only names the two decisions); (3) a useRef mirror of `rows` (Codebase Pattern #82/#106) prevents stale-closure reads inside the subscription callback without forcing re-subscription on every list mutation; (4) mobile-mcp captured us-044-applied-pending-empty.png + us-044-applied-approved-empty.png proving the subscription wiring doesn't crash the screen under the DevRolePicker creator session (subscription is no-op there because the dev token is a literal string, not a signed JWT — same posture as US-036/US-043 per Codebase Pattern #122); (5) scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts two-mode probe (signup | subscribe) that mints a real Marketify JWT via auth-signup-creator, calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(jwt), subscribes to UPDATE events under the same RLS policy (applications_creator_rw, polcmd='*') that guards the in-app stream, and asserts payload.new.status matches expected — ran end-to-end this iteration and returned FAIL with '[channel] status=TIMED_OUT' repeated four times because the broker never reached SUBSCRIBED. Probe test fixture (user_id a3495acd-7241-4d41-8e15-d42a210fdf65 + cascading rows) was cleaned up immediately via execute_sql. Code-reviewer returned 2 IMPORTANT findings, both carried over unchanged from US-036 (stale-token-at-subscription-time baseline across all realtime screens) and both AC-conformant (toast fires only on approved/rejected, matching the exact AC wording). Next iteration should re-run the probe (bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts signup then subscribe) once Supabase Realtime tenant is healthy — if verdict PASS, flip passes:true. Tenant hibernation/cold-start is the likely root cause; waiting for the tenant to warm up + checking get_logs({service:'realtime'}) for 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (the postgres_changes slot, not just the messages slot) before running the probe is the recommended order."
}

content.content:
1	import { Redirect, Slot } from 'expo-router';
2	import { Building2, Inbox, LayoutDashboard, Megaphone } from 'lucide-react-native';
3	import { StyleSheet, View } from 'react-native';
4	import { TabBar, type TabDef } from '@/components/shared/TabBar';
5	import { colors } from '@/design/tokens';
6	import { useAuth } from '@/lib/auth';
7	
8	// Lister tab group — Home (index) / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile per US-031 AC
9	// + docs/design.md §3.2. Same custom-TabBar rationale as the creator layout:
10	// expo-router's native `<Tabs>` crashes in Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch. The FAB
11	// is mounted on the Home screen per §3.2 ("Create-campaign is a FAB on
12	// Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump").
13	const LISTER_TABS: TabDef[] = [
14	  {
15	    label: 'Home',
16	    icon: LayoutDashboard,
17	    path: '/(lister)/dashboard',
18	    activePathname: '/dashboard',
19	    testID: 'tab-dashboard',
20	  },
21	  {
22	    label: 'Campaigns',
23	    icon: Megaphone,
24	    path: '/(lister)/campaigns',
25	    activePathname: '/campaigns',
26	    testID: 'tab-campaigns',
27	  },
28	  {
29	    label: 'Inbox',
30	    icon: Inbox,
31	    path: '/(lister)/inbox',
32	    activePathname: '/inbox',
33	    testID: 'tab-inbox',
34	  },
35	  {
36	    label: 'Profile',
37	    icon: Building2,
38	    path: '/(lister)/profile',
39	    activePathname: '/profile',
40	    testID: 'tab-profile',
41	  },
42	];
43	
44	export default function ListerLayout() {
45	  const { role } = useAuth();
46	  if (role !== 'lister') return <Redirect href="/(auth)" />;
47	
48	  return (
49	    <View style={styles.root}>
50	      <View style={styles.content}>
51	        <Slot />
52	      </View>
53	      <TabBar tabs={LISTER_TABS} />
54	    </View>
55	  );
56	}
57	
58	const styles = StyleSheet.create({
59	  root: { flex: 1, backgroundColor: colors.canvas },
60	  content: { flex: 1 },
61	});
62

content.content:
164:| Dashboard | `/(lister)/dashboard` | Stat tiles + recent activity | "Create campaign" (FAB) |
165-| Create Campaign — Steps 1–5 | `/(lister)/campaigns/new/*` | Wizard: basics → price → pre → post → samples → review | "Next" / "Publish" |
166-| My Campaigns | `/(lister)/campaigns` | List active + inactive | Tap card |
167-| Campaign Detail (lister) | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]` | Manage: toggle active, edit, counts | "Edit" / Toggle |
168-| Edit Campaign | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit` | Modify fields (triggers cancel-warning) | "Save changes" |
169-| Applications Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` | Review applicants with metrics | Tap row |
170-| Application Review | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications/[appId]` | Approve/Reject | "Approve" / "Reject" |
171-| Submissions Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions` | Review final videos | Tap row |
172-| Submission Review | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions/[subId]` | Video + checklist + decide | "Approve" / "Reject" |
173-| Profile | `/(lister)/profile` | Company profile | "Edit" |
174-| Notifications | `/(lister)/notifications` | Activity inbox | Tap item |
175-
176-### 2.4 Shared / System
177-
178-| Screen | Route | Role | Purpose |
179-|---|---|---|---|
180-| Status Detail (auto-cancel) | `/shared/status/cancelled/[id]` | C | Explains what changed when a listing got edited |
181-| Error | `/shared/error` | S | Generic recoverable error screen |
182-| Not Found | `/shared/404` | S | Dead-link fallback |
183-
184-### 2.5 Empty & Error States (inline)
185-
186-- Feed empty (no campaigns): "Nothing live right now." + "Notify me" toggle.
187-- Feed empty (no eligible): see §5.5.
188-- Applications empty: "Apply to your first campaign." → feed.
189-- Submissions empty: "No submissions yet. Get approved on an application to start filming."
190-- Lister dashboard empty: "Post your first bounty."
191-- Applications inbox empty (lister): "Waiting for creators to apply."
192-- Network error: hard-shadow card with "Can't reach servers" + "Try again".
193-
194-**Age-bucketed nudges for non-expiring inboxes.** Applications and submissions do not auto-expire, so an inbox is never "full" in the OS sense — but stale rows rot quietly. On inboxes where any open row is older than a threshold, the list prepends a soft-warning banner (not an empty state — the list is non-empty):
195-
196-| Inbox | Trigger | Banner copy |
197-|---|---|---|
198-| Lister Applications Inbox | any pending application `created_at > 7 days` | *"N applications pending > 7 days. Review or close the listing."* |
199-| Lister Submissions Inbox | any pending submission `created_at > 5 days` | *"N submissions waiting > 5 days. Creators are watching the clock."* |
200-| Creator Applications | any pending application `created_at > 14 days` and no activity | *"N applications no one's touched in 2 weeks. Find a similar bounty?"* → routes to Feed |
201-
202-Banner is dismissible per-session but returns on next cold start until the condition clears.
203-
204----
--
223:| Dashboard | layout-dashboard | Home | `/(lister)/dashboard` |
224-| Campaigns | megaphone | Campaigns | `/(lister)/campaigns` |
225-| Inbox | inbox | Inbox | `/(lister)/inbox` (apps + submissions, segmented) |
226-| Profile | building-2 | Profile | `/(lister)/profile` |
227-
228:Create-campaign is a **FAB** (cta color, hard shadow, 56px) on Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump.
229-
230-**Inbox model — relationship to §2.3 IA.** §2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up — same underlying data, different entry point:
231-
232-- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).
233-- **Default segment:** whichever has the higher unreviewed count; ties go to Submissions (higher creator-anxiety cost per day of delay).
234-- **Row grouping:** grouped by campaign with `SectionHeader` rows; tapping a row pushes into the campaign-scoped review screen (same route as §2.3).
235-- **Empty state:** if both segments empty, "Nothing to review. Good time to post another bounty." with a CTA button to Create Campaign.
236-
237-So the campaign-scoped routes are the sources of truth; the Inbox tab is a lens. This matches how lister work actually happens — sometimes you think "that Nike campaign has applicants" (campaign-first), sometimes "do I have anything to review?" (inbox-first).
238-
239-### 3.3 Modal vs Stack vs Tab
240-
241-| Case | Pattern | Why |
242-|---|---|---|
243-| Filters (creator feed) | Bottom sheet (80% height) | Scannable, preserves feed scroll. |
244-| Apply Confirm | Full-screen modal slide-up | Commitment moment — needs focus. |
245-| Create Campaign wizard | Stack with progress header | 5 steps need back-navigation. |
246-| Edit Campaign | Full-screen modal | Single-page edit. |
247-| Application Review (lister) | Stack push | Back-swipe preserved. |
248-| Submission Review (lister) | Stack push with video | Back-swipe preserved. |
249-| Submission Composer | Stack push | Mid-flow from application detail. |
250-| Confirmations | Alert-style modal | Short, high-stakes. |
251-| Notifications | Stack push from bell | Allows deep-link. |
252-
253-### 3.4 Deep Link Strategy
254-
255-Scheme: `marketify://`
256-
257-| Event | Deep link | Lands on | Back-stack seed |
258-|---|---|---|---|
259-| Creator app approved | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail with animated status change | Applied tab → Application Detail |
260-| Creator app rejected | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail, rejection reason expanded | Applied tab → Application Detail |
261-| Creator app cancelled (listing edited) | `marketify://applications/:id?reason=listing_edited` | Application Detail with "What changed" diff card | Applied tab → Application Detail |
262-| Submission approved/rejected | `marketify://submissions/:id` | Submission Detail with status change | Submitted tab → Submission Detail |
263-| Lister new application | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/applications/:aid` | Application Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Applications Inbox → Application Review |
264-| Lister new submission | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/submissions/:sid` | Submission Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Submissions Inbox → Submission Review |
265-| Metric refresh ready | `marketify://profile` | Profile with refresh banner | Profile tab (own root) |
266-
267:Built with `expo-router`. **Back-stack seeding rule:** deep links hydrate a realistic navigation spine, not just the target screen on top of the home tab. The table above spells out each link's spine — back-swipe from an Application Review lands the lister on the Applications Inbox they would have reached manually, not on Dashboard. One-level-deep links (creator Application Detail, Profile) seed a single parent tab and stop there.
268-
269----
270-
271-## 4. Key Screen Wireframes (text-described)
272-
273-All measurements assume a 390×844 iPhone baseline. 16px horizontal gutter. 12px vertical rhythm. Safe-area respected.
274-
275-### 4.0 Onboarding — Fetching Metrics (Creator)
276-
277-```
278-┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
279-│                                           │
280-│                  ▓▓▓▓▓                    │ animated geometric
281-│                 ▓     ▓                   │ line illustration
282-│                 ▓     ▓                   │ (primary + ink)
283-│                  ▓▓▓▓▓                    │
284-│                                           │
285-│         Fetching your stats               │ Clash Display 22
286-│                                           │
287-│   We're pulling your public profile(s).   │ Satoshi 15
288-│   Usually takes 15–60 seconds.            │
289-│                                           │
290-│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │
291-│   │ ● TikTok       @sarahfilms   ✓ 18s │ │ per-run status row
292-│   │ ● Instagram    @sarahfilms   …     │ │ (ig_details)
293-│   │ ● Instagram — videos         …     │ │ (ig_posts)
294-│   └─────────────────────────────────────┘ │
295-│                                           │
296-│   [ Keep me here ]  [ Continue in bg → ]  │ Secondary / Primary
297-│                                           │
298-└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
299-```
300-

content.content:
160:### 2.3 Lister Screens
164:| Dashboard | `/(lister)/dashboard` | Stat tiles + recent activity | "Create campaign" (FAB) |
190:- Lister dashboard empty: "Post your first bounty."
198:| Lister Applications Inbox | any pending application `created_at > 7 days` | *"N applications pending > 7 days. Review or close the listing."* |
199:| Lister Submissions Inbox | any pending submission `created_at > 5 days` | *"N submissions waiting > 5 days. Creators are watching the clock."* |
219:### 3.2 Tab Bar — Lister (4 tabs)
223:| Dashboard | layout-dashboard | Home | `/(lister)/dashboard` |
228:Create-campaign is a **FAB** (cta color, hard shadow, 56px) on Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump.
263:| Lister new application | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/applications/:aid` | Application Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Applications Inbox → Application Review |
264:| Lister new submission | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/submissions/:sid` | Submission Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Submissions Inbox → Submission Review |
267:Built with `expo-router`. **Back-stack seeding rule:** deep links hydrate a realistic navigation spine, not just the target screen on top of the home tab. The table above spells out each link's spine — back-swipe from an Application Review lands the lister on the Applications Inbox they would have reached manually, not on Dashboard. One-level-deep links (creator Application Detail, Profile) seed a single parent tab and stop there.
421:│    │ sarahfilms                         │  │ (same style as §4.7)
518:### 4.5 Create Campaign Wizard (Lister)
549:### 4.6 Submission Review (Lister)
607:**Self-affirm vs review checkbox mapping.** The Submission Composer (creator, §4.4) and Submission Review (lister, §4.6) render the same `ConditionChecklist` with different `mode` props:
625:### 5.2 Application Auto-Cancellation (Lister Edited Listing)
635:- **Lister side:** "This will cancel **N** pending applications. They'll be notified." Requires explicit confirmation button.
636:- **Lister-side warning on subsequent edits:** after the first version bump, every subsequent edit confirmation also surfaces: *"Creators cancelled by a previous edit are **not** re-notified. Only currently active applications (N) will be told about this change."* This is a one-way fan-out — by design, to prevent spamming ex-applicants — but listers have expected "edit again = re-ping everyone" and been surprised. Copy is inline in the ConfirmationModal body, not a separate dialog, so it reads as context, not another obstacle.
654:- Lister feedback card always visible (danger-soft bg).
724:| **FAB** | Floating action (Lister) | `icon, onPress` |
767:- [ ] Body text contrast verified ≥ 4.5:1 (`#0F172A` on `#FFF9F2` = 17.8:1, AAA)

lastPrompt: # Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions  You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Eac…

content.text: Now let me verify the enum names and existing grant posture.

input.query:
-- Verify enum names exist
select n.nspname, t.typname
from pg_type t
join pg_namespace n on n.oid = t.typnamespace
where n.nspname = 'public'
  and t.typname in ('listing_status', 'application_status', 'submission_status')
order by t.typname;

input.query:
-- Check grants on the three tables to authenticated role
select grantee, table_name, privilege_type
from information_schema.role_table_grants
where table_schema = 'public'
  and grantee = 'authenticated'
  and table_name in ('listings', 'applications', 'submissions')
order by table_name, privilege_type;

1	// Typography — typed text-style presets for the Marketify design system.
2	// Mirrors docs/design.md §1.3 (font pairing table + line-height rules).
3	//
4	// Font substitution: docs/design.md names Clash Display (headings) and Satoshi (body).
5	// Both are Fontshare-hosted and do not ship with Expo Google Fonts; we use the explicit
6	// fallbacks listed in §1.3 — Outfit for display/headings, Rubik for body/caption — plus
7	// JetBrains Mono for monospace. Spec-sanctioned per the same section.
8	//
9	// `fontFamily` values are the string keys registered with `expo-font.useFonts` in
10	// `app/_layout.tsx`. They MUST match the constant names imported from
11	// `@expo-google-fonts/*` so RN can resolve the loaded assets by name.
12	
13	import {
14	  Outfit_600SemiBold,
15	  Outfit_700Bold,
16	} from '@expo-google-fonts/outfit';
17	import {
18	  Rubik_500Medium,
19	  Rubik_700Bold,
20	} from '@expo-google-fonts/rubik';
21	import { JetBrainsMono_500Medium } from '@expo-google-fonts/jetbrains-mono';
22	import type { TextStyle } from 'react-native';
23	
24	export const fontFamilies = {
25	  displayBold: 'Outfit_700Bold',
26	  displaySemi: 'Outfit_600SemiBold',
27	  bodyMedium: 'Rubik_500Medium',
28	  bodyBold: 'Rubik_700Bold',
29	  mono: 'JetBrainsMono_500Medium',
30	} as const;
31	
32	// Map consumed by `useFonts` — values are the numeric asset handles exported by
33	// @expo-google-fonts/*, keys are the runtime font-family names.
34	export const fontAssets = {
35	  [fontFamilies.displayBold]: Outfit_700Bold,
36	  [fontFamilies.displaySemi]: Outfit_600SemiBold,
37	  [fontFamilies.bodyMedium]: Rubik_500Medium,
38	  [fontFamilies.bodyBold]: Rubik_700Bold,
39	  [fontFamilies.mono]: JetBrainsMono_500Medium,
40	} as const;
41	
42	// Line heights per §1.3: 1.15 for display, ~1.55 for body. Caption + mono tighten
43	// to 1.4 for dense data rows.
44	export const textStyles = {
45	  display: {
46	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.displayBold,
47	    fontSize: 28,
48	    lineHeight: 32,
49	  },
50	  h1: {
51	    fontFamily: fontFamilies.displaySemi,
52	    fontSize: 22,
53	    lineHeight: 25,

76	  // 0.08em tracking at 11pt = 0.88pt (RN letterSpacing is absolute points).
77	  micro: {

79	    fontSize: 11,
80	    lineHeight: 14,
81	    letterSpacing: 0.88,
82	    textTransform: 'uppercase',
83	  },

input.content:
-- US-048 — Lister Home/dashboard screen counts RPC.
--
-- Contract: `public.lister_dashboard_counts()` returns a single row with
-- three integer columns: active_campaigns, pending_applications,
-- pending_submissions. Called by app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx to populate the
-- stat tiles per docs/design.md §2.3 (Dashboard = "Stat tiles + recent
-- activity", v1 scope = counts only per docs/product-plan.md line 124).
--
-- Why a SECURITY INVOKER RPC instead of three PostgREST count queries:
-- (1) single round-trip, atomic numbers rendered together — no race where
--     the two counts disagree because they were fetched 20ms apart; (2)
--     typed via generated Supabase types (Functions.lister_dashboard_counts)
--     so the caller gets column names, not row counts pulled out of a
--     Range header; (3) SECURITY INVOKER preserves RLS — the three policy
--     gates (`listings_lister_all`, `applications_lister_read`,
--     `submissions_lister_read`) still apply, AND we repeat the
--     `lister_id = public.current_user_id()` filter as a belt-and-suspenders
--     guarantee so a future RLS regression doesn't silently inflate the
--     counts with other listers' rows.
--
-- Relationship to Codebase Patterns:
-- - Pattern #119 naming convention: `lister_dashboard_counts` is a
--   caller-scoped read; argumentless (caller implicit via
--   current_user_id()); lives in `public` so PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`
--   can reach it.
-- - Pattern #42: `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify everything, even
--   though SQL functions don't have the PL/pgSQL GREATEST gotcha; enum
--   casts are written `'active'::public.listing_status` so they resolve
--   correctly under the empty search_path.
-- - Pattern #39: reuses `public.current_user_id()` (reads the `sub` claim
--   off auth.jwt()) — same mechanism every caller-scoped query uses.

create or replace function public.lister_dashboard_counts()
returns table(
  active_campaigns integer,
  pending_applications integer,
  pending_submissions integer
)
language sql
stable
security invoker
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select
    (
      select count(*)::integer
      from public.listings l
      where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
        and l.status = 'active'::public.listing_status
    ) as active_campaigns,
    (
      select count(*)::integer
      from public.applications a
      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
      where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
        and a.status = 'pending'::public.application_status
    ) as pending_applications,
    (
      select count(*)::integer
      from public.submissions s
      join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
      where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
        and s.status = 'pending'::public.submission_status
    ) as pending_submissions
$$;

revoke all on function public.lister_dashboard_counts() from public;
revoke all on function public.lister_dashboard_counts() from anon;
grant execute on function public.lister_dashboard_counts() to authenticated;

input.query:
-- Cross-check: what listers exist in users + what listings they own
select u.id, u.username, u.role,
       (select count(*) from public.listings where lister_id = u.id) as lcount,
       (select count(*) from public.listings where lister_id = u.id and status='active') as active_count
from public.users u
where u.role = 'lister'
order by u.id;

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user invoked a Ralph autonomous loop iteration for the "Marketify" project — a mobile marketplace MVP (Expo + React Native + Supabase) where creators claim bounties from listers. Per `scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md`, my task is to:
   - Orient via `scripts/ralph/prd.json` + `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`
   - Pick ONE story with the lowest `priority` where `passes:false`
   - Implement the minimal change satisfying every acceptance criterion
   - Run quality gates (tsc, lint, migration, advisors)
   - Run feature-dev:code-reviewer subagent
   - Run mobile-mcp verification for UI stories
   - Commit, mark `passes:true` in prd.json, append to progress.txt
   - If all stories pass, emit `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`; otherwise end normally
   
   The user did not issue explicit messages beyond the system initialization — this is fully autonomous Ralph loop execution.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Expo Router v6 with custom TabBar (expo-router `<Tabs>` crashes in Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch)
   - Supabase MCP for migrations, SQL execution, type generation, advisors, logs
   - Custom Marketify JWT (MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET) injected via `marketifyFetch` global fetch override; Supabase Auth's `persistSession/autoRefreshToken/detectSessionInUrl` all false
   - `app_role` JWT claim (distinct from Postgres `role` claim) drives RLS helpers `current_user_id()` / `current_user_role()`
   - PL/pgSQL + SQL function hardening: `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify all refs
   - SECURITY DEFINER vs INVOKER RPCs (Pattern #118)
   - Screen-RPC naming convention: `list_my_*` for caller-scoped reads, `<action>_rpc` for mutations, `<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates (Pattern #119)
   - Supabase Realtime tenant hibernation / postgres_changes replication slot diagnostics (Pattern #124)
   - Neubrutalist design tokens: `colors`, `shadows.hard` (offset 3,3, opacity 1, radius 0)
   - Lucide icons only, no emoji in UI
   - Mobile-mcp iOS simulator verification required for UI stories
   - Migrations append-only, named `us_NNN_description`

3. Files and Code Sections:

     revoke all on function public.lister_dashboard_counts() from anon;

-- US-048 — Lister Home/dashboard screen counts RPC.
--
-- Contract: `public.lister_dashboard_counts()` returns a single row with
-- three integer columns: active_campaigns, pending_applications,
-- pending_submissions. Called by app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx to populate the
-- stat tiles per docs/design.md §2.3 (Dashboard = "Stat tiles + recent
-- activity", v1 scope = counts only per docs/product-plan.md line 124).
--
-- Why a SECURITY INVOKER RPC instead of three PostgREST count queries:
-- (1) single round-trip, atomic numbers rendered together — no race where
--     the two counts disagree because they were fetched 20ms apart; (2)
--     typed via generated Supabase types (Functions.lister_dashboard_counts)
--     so the caller gets column names, not row counts pulled out of a
--     Range header; (3) SECURITY INVOKER preserves RLS — the three policy
--     gates (`listings_lister_all`, `applications_lister_read`,
--     `submissions_lister_read`) still apply, AND we repeat the
--     `lister_id = public.current_user_id()` filter as a belt-and-suspenders
--     guarantee so a future RLS regression doesn't silently inflate the
--     counts with other listers' rows.
--
-- Relationship to Codebase Patterns:
-- - Pattern #119 naming convention: `lister_dashboard_counts` is a
--   caller-scoped read; argumentless (caller implicit via
--   current_user_id()); lives in `public` so PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`
--   can reach it.
-- - Pattern #42: `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify everything, even
--   though SQL functions don't have the PL/pgSQL GREATEST gotcha; enum
--   casts are written `'active'::public.listing_status` so they resolve
--   correctly under the empty search_path.
-- - Pattern #39: reuses `public.current_user_id()` (reads the `sub` claim
--   off auth.jwt()) — same mechanism every caller-scoped query uses.

create or replace function public.lister_dashboard_counts()
returns table(
  active_campaigns integer,
  pending_applications integer,
  pending_submissions integer
)
language sql
stable
security invoker
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select
    (
      select count(*)::integer
      from public.listings l
      where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
        and l.status = 'active'::public.listing_status
    ) as active_campaigns,
    (
      select count(*)::integer
      from public.applications a
      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
      where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
        and a.status = 'pending'::public.application_status
    ) as pending_applications,
    (
      select count(*)::integer
      from public.submissions s
      join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
      where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
        and s.status = 'pending'::public.submission_status
    ) as pending_submissions
$$;

revoke all on function public.lister_dashboard_counts() from public;
revoke all on function public.lister_dashboard_counts() from anon;
grant execute on function public.lister_dashboard_counts() to authenticated;

# Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Each invocation is a fresh Claude Code instance with no prior memory. Every persistent decision lives in git history, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, and `scripts/ralph/prd.json`.

---

## Authoritative specs (read these for any ambiguity)

| File | What it defines |
|---|---|
| `docs/product-plan.md` | Canonical product, 12-step build sequence, scope cuts |
| `docs/design.md` | Visual language, component inventory, screen catalog, motion |
| `docs/tech-architecture.md` | DB schema (§4.7), RLS, triggers, Apify integration (§3), edge-function spec, eligibility engine |

If a story is ambiguous, the specs win. Do **not** invent product behavior the docs don't sanction. If something is genuinely missing, append a `Spec gap:` note to `progress.txt` and pick the simplest interpretation that aligns with the docs.

---

## The loop you run, every iteration

1. **Orient.** Read `scripts/ralph/prd.json` and `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`. Pay special attention to the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt` — it consolidates everything previous iterations learned.
2. **Branch.** Make sure you're on the branch named in `prd.json.branchName`. If it doesn't exist, create it from `main`.
3. **Pick one story.** Lowest `priority` number where `passes: false`. Work on **exactly one** story per iteration.
4. **Research before coding.** If understanding the story requires reading >3 files, **delegate to the Explore subagent** (`subagent_type: "Explore"`) so your own context stays clean for implementation. Pass it the story plus pointers into the docs.
5. **Implement.** Make the minimal change set that satisfies every acceptance criterion. Match existing conventions. Don't refactor adjacent code.
6. **Quality gates** (run in this order, stop on first failure and fix before continuing):
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` — must pass with zero errors
   - `npx expo lint` — must pass (warnings allowed only if pre-existing in main)
   - Migration sanity: any new SQL must apply cleanly via Supabase MCP without manual fix-up
7. **Code review (mandatory before commit).** Spawn the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with the diff scope, the story acceptance criteria, and the relevant doc sections. Address every issue rated medium or higher. Re-run quality gates after fixes.
8. **Mobile verification (mandatory for any UI story).** Use `mobile-mcp` to:
   - Launch the app on the iOS simulator (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_launch_app`)
   - Navigate to the screen the story touches
   - Exercise the golden path
   - Take a screenshot (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_take_screenshot`) and reference it in the progress note
   - If the simulator is unavailable, **do not silently skip** — set `passes: false`, write a `notes` entry on the story explaining the blocker, and move to the next story.
9. **Commit.** Stage everything you changed (no `git add -A` blanket — list paths). Commit message format:
   ```
   feat: [US-XXX] Story title
   ```
10. **Mark done.** In `prd.json`, set `passes: true` for the story you just finished. Append a dated entry to `progress.txt` (format below).
11. **Stop check.** If every story has `passes: true`, your final response must contain `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` and nothing else useful. Otherwise end normally — the next iteration picks up.

---

## MCP servers — use these instead of CLI wherever possible

### Supabase (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`) — required for ALL database work
- `apply_migration` — every schema change. Migrations are append-only and named after the story (e.g. `us_006_metric_snapshots_denorm_trigger`).
- `execute_sql` — read-only queries, RLS verification, seed data inspection.
- `list_tables`, `list_migrations`, `list_extensions` — orient before changing things.
- `generate_typescript_types` — regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts` after every migration. Commit the regenerated file with the migration.
- `deploy_edge_function`, `get_edge_function`, `list_edge_functions` — for `apify-core`, `auth-*`, `metrics-refresh`, the webhook receiver, etc.
- `get_advisors` — run with `type: "security"` after any schema or RLS change. Address every error-level advisor before committing.
- `get_logs` — when an edge function or query misbehaves.

Do **not** use the local `supabase` CLI for migrations or queries. The MCP is the source of truth so iterations stay consistent.

### Mobile (`mcp__mobile-mcp__*`) — required for any UI verification
- `mobile_list_available_devices` then `mobile_launch_app` to boot the app.
- `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` + `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` to drive flows.
- `mobile_take_screenshot` after each meaningful state — name screenshots `us-XXX-state.png` and store under `scripts/ralph/screenshots/`.
- Use `mobile_type_keys` rather than tapping virtual keys when filling text inputs.

### Context7 (`mcp__plugin_context7_context7__*`) — for any library docs
Use **before** writing non-trivial integration code with Expo, Reanimated, NativeWind, Supabase JS, React Navigation, etc. Your training data may be stale. `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs`.

### Apify
The API key is in `.env` as `APIFY_KEY`. Always read it at runtime via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` (or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` inside edge functions). **Never** paste the key into source. Actor catalog and input schemas live in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §3.

---

## Subagent delegation — preserve your context

You will burn out your own context window if you try to do everything in-line. Delegate aggressively:

| When | Subagent | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Need to read >3 files to understand the codebase | `Explore` | Returns a focused summary instead of dumping raw file contents into your context |
| Before every commit | `feature-dev:code-reviewer` | Independent review against acceptance criteria + doc sections |
| Implementation will touch >5 files (e.g. wiring a new edge function end-to-end) | `feature-dev:code-architect` first, then implement yourself | Plan once, execute once |
| Need to research how something works before changing it | `feature-dev:code-explorer` | Maps the existing layers without polluting your context |
| Open-ended search across the repo | `general-purpose` | Iterates until it finds the answer |

When briefing a subagent, give it the story ID, acceptance criteria, and a pointer to the relevant `docs/*.md` section. Do **not** tell it "based on your findings, implement" — synthesize the result yourself.

---

## Project quality conventions (these become permanent — additions go in `progress.txt` Codebase Patterns)

- **TypeScript everywhere.** No `any` without a `// eslint-disable-next-line` and a comment explaining why. Generated Supabase types are imported from `src/types/supabase.ts`.
- **Migrations are append-only.** Never edit a previously applied migration. If you need to change something, write a new migration that alters the prior state.
- **Edge functions** live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`. Each one has a one-paragraph header comment naming the contract (request shape → response shape) and the auth requirement.
- **RLS is mandatory** on every table that holds user data. After applying any RLS migration, run `get_advisors({type: "security"})` and fix every finding before committing.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific composites in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji** anywhere in the product UI (per `docs/design.md` — Lucide icons only).
- **No payments code** in v1 — `price_cents` is display-only.
- **Dual-role accounts** are out of scope for v1. One role per account.

---

## Progress log format (`progress.txt`)

Append (never replace) using this format:

```
## YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — US-XXX
- What was implemented (1–3 bullets, concrete)
- Files changed (bulleted list)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: [approved | issues fixed in this commit]
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered
  - Gotchas
  - Useful pointers
---
```

If a learning is **general and reusable across stories**, also promote it into the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt`. That section is what every future iteration leans on first — keep it tight, deduplicated, and signal-only.

---

## Stop condition

After updating `prd.json`, count remaining `passes: false` stories. If zero, your last line in this response must be exactly:

```
<promise>COMPLETE</promise>
```

Otherwise end normally and let the loop spawn the next iteration.

---

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **One story per iteration.** Don't peek ahead.
- **Never commit broken code.** A failing typecheck means you don't commit, you don't mark `passes: true`, you write a `notes` entry, and the next iteration retries.
- **Never skip the code-reviewer subagent.** It is the second pair of eyes that keeps quality from drifting across iterations.
- **Never skip mobile verification on UI stories.** A screen "looking right in the JSX" is not the same as it actually rendering correctly.
- **Never read `.env` contents into the conversation.** Reference variable names only.
- **Never edit `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, or `docs/tech-architecture.md`** unless a story explicitly says to. They are specs, not work product.
- **Never invent stories** or add new ones to `prd.json` mid-loop. If you discover a missing piece, append it as a `Spec gap:` line in `progress.txt` so the human can decide.

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.
